evidence_summary: Establish the discovery of a massive gold-bearing quartz vein in the Arizona hills to set the stakes for the narrative. The scene must transition from the high energy of discovery to the sober reality of securing the claim through a logistical plan.
In 1865, Carter and Powell discover a massive gold-bearing quartz vein in the Arizona hills. They agree on a plan: Powell will head to civilization for machinery while Carter stays to guard the site.
Carter and Powell stand before a newly revealed, shimmering gold-bearing quartz vein under bright sun.
The men stand apart, one preparing to depart and the other remaining to guard the site.
Discovery of wealth leads to a strategic division of labor between guarding and procurement.
